title: Using the lens formula
description: Let's practice some problems to apply and better understand the lens formula for convex and concave lenses.

assessment_items.question_content:
A convex lens has a focal length of $10\ \text{cm}$. 

Let's place an object $5.0\ \text{cm}$ from it.

**What is the image distance, $v$?**   
*Let's use the Cartesian sign convention.*

$v=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

In the Cartesian sign convention, we have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-5.0\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=+10\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The image distance, $v$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{v}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}+\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{+10\ \text{cm}})}+\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-5.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1-2}{10\ \text{cm}}\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{10\ \text{cm}}\\\\
v&=-10\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$v=\boxed{-10}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed in front of a convex lens of focal length $8.0\ \text{cm}$. 

A virtual image forms $2.0\ \text{cm}$ from the lens.

**What is the object distance, $u$?**   
*Note: Answer using the Cartesian sign convention.*

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

Since virtual images form on the same side as the object, the image distance is negative in the Cartesian sign convention. We have -

- image distance, $\redE v=-2.0\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=+8.0\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The object distance, $u$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{u}&=\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-2.0\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{8.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-4.0-1.0}{8.0\ \text{cm}}\\\\
&=-\dfrac{5.0}{8.0\ \text{cm}}\\\\
u&=-\dfrac{8.0}{5.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=-1.6\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$u=\boxed{-1.6}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A virtual image forms $14\ \text{cm}$ from a convex lens when an object is kept at a distance of $4.0\ \text{cm}$ from it.

**What is the focal length, $f$, of the lens?**   
*Note: Answer using the Cartesian sign convention.*

$f=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

Since virtual images form on the same side as the object, the image distance is negative in the Cartesian sign convention. We have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-4.0\ \text{cm}$,

- image distance, $\blueE v=-14\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The focal length, $f$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{f}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{f}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{-14\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-4.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-2.0+7.0}{28\ \text{cm}}\\\\
f&=\dfrac{28}{5.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=5.6\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$u=\boxed{+5.6}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A candle is placed $9.0\ \text{cm}$ from a convex lens of focal length $6.0\ \text{cm}$.

**What is the image distance, $v$?**   
*Let's use the Cartesian sign convention.*

$v=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

In the Cartesian sign convention, we have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-9.0\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=+6.0\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The image distance, $v$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{v}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}+\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{+6.0\ \text{cm}})}+\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-9.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3.0-2.0}{18\ \text{cm}}\\\\
v&=+18\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$v=\boxed{+18}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
When an object is held in front of a convex lens of focal length $5.0\ \text{cm}$, a real image forms at a distance of $15\ \text{cm}$ from it.

**What is the object distance, $u$?**   
*Let's use the Cartesian sign convention.*

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

In the Cartesian sign convention, we have -

- image distance, $\redE v=+15\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=+5.0\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The object distance, $u$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{u}&=\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\redE{+15\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{+5.0\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1.0-3.0}{15\ \text{cm}}\\\\
u&=-\dfrac{15}{2.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=-7.5\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$v=\boxed{-7.5}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A real image forms $12\ \text{cm}$ from a convex lens when an object is kept $18\ \text{cm}$ from it.

**What is the focal length, $f$, of the lens?**   
*Note: Answer using the Cartesian sign convention.*

$f=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

Since virtual images form on the same side as the object, the image distance is negative in the Cartesian sign convention. We have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-18\ \text{cm}$,

- image distance, $\blueE v=+12\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The focal length, $f$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{f}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{f}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{+12\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-18\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3.0+2.0}{36\ \text{cm}}\\\\
f&=\dfrac{36}{5.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=7.2\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$f=\boxed{+7.2}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A candle is placed $21\ \text{cm}$ from a concave lens of focal length $7.0\ \text{cm}$.

**What is the image distance, $v$?**   
*Let's use the Cartesian sign convention.*

$v=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

In the Cartesian sign convention, we have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-21\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=-7.0\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The image distance, $v$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{v}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}+\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{-7.0\ \text{cm}})}+\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-21\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3.0-1.0}{21\ \text{cm}}\\\\
v&=-\dfrac{21}{4.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=-5.25\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$v=\boxed{-5.25}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
An object is placed in front of a concave lens of focal length $10\ \text{cm}$. A virtual image forms $2.0\ \text{cm}$ from the lens.

**What is the object distance, $u$?**   
*Let's use the Cartesian sign convention.*

$u=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

In the Cartesian sign convention, we have -

- image distance, $\redE v=-2.0\ \text{cm}$,

- focal length, $\blueE f=-10\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The object distance, $u$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{u}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{u}&=\dfrac{1}{\redE v}-\dfrac{1}{\blueE f}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-2.0\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{-10\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-5.0+1.0}{10\ \text{cm}}\\\\
u&=-\dfrac{10}{4.0}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=-2.5\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$v=\boxed{-2.5}\ \text{cm}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A virtual image forms $3.0\ \text{cm}$ from a concave lens when an object is kept $13\ \text{cm}$ from it.

**What is the focal length, $f$, of the lens?**   
*Note: Answer using the Cartesian sign convention.*

$f=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
##Strategy

Since virtual images form on the same side as the object, the image distance is negative in the Cartesian sign convention. We have -

- object distance, $\redE u=-13\ \text{cm}$,

- image distance, $\blueE v=-3.0\ \text{cm}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

The focal length, $f$, can be found using the **lens formula**,

$\begin{align}
\boxed{\dfrac{1}{f}=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}}
\end{align}$

##Solution

From the lens formula, we have,

$\begin{align}
\dfrac{1}{f}&=\dfrac{1}{\blueE v}-\dfrac{1}{\redE u}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(\blueE{-3.0\ \text{cm}})}-\dfrac{1}{(\redE{-13\ \text{cm}})}\\\\
&=\dfrac{-13+3.0}{39\ \text{cm}}\\\\
f&=-\dfrac{39}{10}\ \text{cm}\\\\
&=-3.9\ \text{cm}
\end{align}$

The correct answer is:

$f=\boxed{-3.9}\ \text{cm}$
